Subscribe to our newsletter Fresh off the launch of AMD's first CPU with dual 3D V-cache, the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2, is a Newegg combo deal that you won't want to miss. This deal brings the 9950X3D2 together with an Asus TUF Gaming X870E-Plus motherboard, 32GB of fast DDR5 RAM from Corsair, and a 2TB WD Black SN7100 SSD, and all for a discounted $1,729.99 right now. ● Check out this combo deal at Newegg That's a huge $441.98 saving compared to buying each product separately and, when you price them up, means you're getting the RAM for the equivalent cost of just $28.01. This kit brings together some of the most expensive components for a new gaming rig, leaving you to put that $441.98 towards other parts like a brand-new GPU. Save 20% ($441.98)Newegg Combo: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 / Asus TUF Gaming X870E-Plus Wifi7 / Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5-6400 (2 x 16GB) / WD Black SN7100 2TB: was $2,171.97 now $1,729.99 at NeweggSave 20% on this combined Newegg PC hardware kit, scoring you AMD's first dual-X3D chip in the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2, along with an Asus TUF Gaming X870E-Plus WiFi7 motherboard, 32GB (2x16GB) of Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400 RAM, and a 2TB WD Black SN7100 SSD.View Deal Let's look at that powerhouse processor first. As our 9950X3D2 review makes clearly, this is not a CPU for normal gamers. This is, in every way, a niche product that pushes the limits of AMD's 3D V-cache technology as it stands today. It's the first AMD X3D chip to offer that boosted L3 cache capacity across both CCDs, and it's now dethroned the 9950X3D as the fastest CPU for gaming, even if the margins are thin.This CPU features 16 cores and 32 threads, with a 5.6 GHz boost clock and a 200W TDP, along with double the L3 cache at 192MB. Those power demands are 30W higher than the 9950X3D, and 70W more than the 9800X3D, which is still the best CPU for most gamers in our opinion. Our CPU benchmarks spell it out clearly: there isn't anything better, but don't expect epoch-changing results. It matches the 9800X3D's performance in-game, while multithreaded performance sits about 4% higher than the 9950X3D. If you want the absolute best all-rounder, the 9950X3D2 is it. Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) The CPU isn't the only component in this deal bundle, though. Fitted with the 9950X3D2 is an Asus TUF Gaming X870E-Plus WiFi 7 motherboard, aimed at enthusiasts with a whole host of features fit for gaming. This AM5 board comes with a total of 13 USBs at the rear, including two USB-C ports rated for 40 Gbps. Four M.2 sockets are included for a whole stack of SSDs, with two rated for PCIe 5.0 over four lanes with the 9800X3D installed. It also has fast networking support built in, with 2.5G and Wi-Fi 7 support included, as well as the ability to install a whopping 256GB of DDR5 RAM over four DIMM slots, with speeds of up to 8,200 MT/s.You aren't quite getting 256GB of DDR5 RAM with this kit, but 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RGB RAM. This Corsair kit includes two 16GB modules, rated at 6,400 MT/s with CL36 latency. This black dual-channel RAM features RGB lighting at the top, which you can set up and customize using the Corsair iCue app. This is fast RAM and, for a new rig, will more than handle the demands of modern gaming.You're also getting a 2TB WD Black SN7100 NVMe M.2 SSD thrown in, too. As our SN7100 review shows, this WD Black SN7100 is a power-efficient drive with PCIe 4.0 speeds, with up to 7,250 MB/s sequential read and 6,900 MB/s sequential write performance. That's only a touch slower than the best SSD offering Gen 4 speeds, the Samsung 990 Pro, with caps at 7,450 and 6,900 MB/s apiece. The cost of both SSDs and RAM has exploded in recent months, thanks to the huge demand pressures caused by AI, so buying this SSD alone would cost you $499.99 right now.The RAM, too, would set you back handsomely: $469.99 for a 32GB kit, not to mention the $899 launch price for the 9950X3D2, as well as $302.99 for this top-spec Asus motherboard. That makes the $1,729.99 sale price for this Newegg combo a serious option if you're planning a new build or upgrade with the 9950X3D2. If you're interested, make sure to grab it with the $441.98 discount offered here before the bundle sells out, as Newegg can pull them without notice. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, Gaming Chair, Best Wi-Fi Routers, Best Motherboard, or CPU Deals pages.
history.author: Ben Stockton
history.titleZh: 在Newegg组合中仅花28美元即可获得32GB海盗船DDR5内存，搭配新款9950X3D2节省441美元——1729.99美元套餐包含AMD首款双X3D CPU、2TB WD Black固态硬盘和华硕TUF Gaming主板
history.descriptionZh:
AMD最新推出的锐龙9 9950X3D2处理器，作为该公司首款配备双3D V-Cache的CPU，刚刚登陆市场便引发关注。Newegg随即推出一款高性价比组合套餐，将这款顶级处理器与华硕TUF Gaming X870E-Plus主板、32GB Corsair DDR5-6400内存以及2TB WD Black SN7100 SSD捆绑销售，总价仅为1,729.99美元，相比单独购买节省了高达441.98美元（折扣20%）。这意味着，你几乎以28.01美元的超低“价格”获得了这套内存，而其他组件则享受了大幅优惠。

**核心技术原理与创新点：双3D V-Cache的极限突破**

锐龙9 9950X3D2的核心创新在于，它是AMD首款将3D V-Cache技术同时应用于两个CCD（核心复合体）的X3D芯片。传统X3D处理器只在单个CCD上堆叠额外缓存，而这款处理器将128MB的3D V-Cache均匀分布在两个CCD上，每个CCD配备64MB，再加上每个CCD原有的32MB L3缓存，使得总L3缓存容量达到惊人的192MB。这一设计大幅提升了跨CCD缓存访问的效率和一致性，对于需要频繁跨核心数据交换的现代游戏和创作应用至关重要。它本质上是AMD为了榨取Zen 5架构最大潜力而推出的“极限版”X3D芯片，旨在弥补此前9950X3D在部分场景下的缓存瓶颈。

**性能参数与对比数据：极致但不激进**

该处理器拥有16核心32线程，最高加速频率5.6GHz，TDP为200W。相比9950X3D（128MB缓存，170W TDP）功耗高出30W，比定位游戏玩家的9800X3D（96MB缓存，120W TDP）高出70W。Tom's Hardware实测显示，在游戏性能方面，9950X3D2与9800X3D基本持平，并未带来革命性提升，但凭借更大缓存和更高时钟频率，它在部分对缓存敏感的游戏中有小幅优势。**在多线程性能上，它比9950X3D领先约4%**，这得益于双CCD缓存带宽的平衡。综合来看，它是目前CPU性能的绝对王者，但更像是一款小众产品——追求极致、不计功耗的发烧友和内容创作者才会真正受益。

**套餐组件深度解析：为高端游戏平台量身定制**

除CPU外，套餐中的其他组件同样定位极客和高端玩家：

*   **主板：华硕TUF Gaming X870E-Plus WiFi 7**：基于AM5平台，配备13个USB接口（含2个40Gbps速率的USB-C），4个M.2 SSD插槽（其中2个在插入9800X3D/9950X3D2后可支持PCIe 5.0 x4）。支持WiFi 7和2.5G有线网卡，最高可扩展至256GB DDR5内存，频率支持最高8200 MT/s。这是为顶级多任务和超频准备的豪华平台。
*   **内存：Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6400 32GB (2x16GB)**：CL36低延迟套件，支持通过Corsair iCue软件定制RGB灯效。6400 MT/s的速度完全能满足现代游戏和高负载应用的带宽需求。
*   **SSD：WD Black SN7100 2TB NVMe M.2**：采用PCIe 4.0接口，顺序读取速度高达7250 MB/s，顺序写入6900 MB/s，略低于三星990 Pro（7450/6900 MB/s），但功耗效率更优。当前AI需求推动存储价格飙升，单独购买这款SSD就需要499.99美元，内存套件也高达469.99美元。

**经济价值与技术影响：高端装机的明智之选**

单独计算，CPU（899美元）+主板（302.99美元）+内存（469.99美元）+SSD（499.99美元）总价为2171.97美元，而套餐仅需1729.99美元，节省了441.98美元。这笔节省的资金足以购买一块性能强劲的独立显卡，使整套配置成为顶级游戏或创作平台的理想选择。

从更广的技术视角看，9950X3D2的定价（899美元）和功耗（200W）显示出AMD正在探索3D V-Cache技术的极致边界。它并非主流选择，但对于工作负载高度依赖于大缓存（如3D渲染、科学计算、AI推理）的用户，或是不计代价追求游戏最高帧率的顶级发烧友，它提供了无可争议的性能天花板。随着AI对存储和内存需求推高价格，这类捆绑套餐在当前市场环境下更具购买价值。由于Newegg可能随时调整库存，有意向的用户需尽快抓住此次441.98美元的折扣机会。

history.title: SoftBank subsidiary working with Intel to develop radical new ZAM memory is now receiving Japanese gov't subsidies — new memory designed as a lower-power HBM for AI workloads
history.description: Japan’s SaiMemory, a SoftBank subsidiary collaborating with Intel, has secured NEDO funding to develop Z-Angle Memory (ZAM), a next-gen DRAM architecture addressing HBM limitations
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter SAIMEMORY, a SoftBank Corp subsidiary in partnership with chip giant Intel, announced that Japan’s New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization (NEDO) had selected its next-generation ZAM memory technology development project for government subsidies that may cover a huge part of the project's development costs.ZAM (Z-Angle Memory) is a potential next-generation alternative to existing AI memory technology, which is designed to be a power-efficient HBM (high-bandwidth memory). It was selected as part of NEDO’s Post-5G Infrastructure Enhancement R&D Project.This news is the latest development in a project shaped by US government-backed research, Intel’s internal R&D, and SoftBank’s push into AI infrastructure. It builds on earlier advances in memory stacking and interconnects from US-led research efforts, with Intel developing key DRAM stacking and bonding techniques that underpin what would become ZAM.Article continues below You may like Intel is co-developing new Z-Angle Memory to compete with HBM used in AI data centers Fujitsu plans dedicated 1.4nm AI chip manufactured entirely in Japan by Rapidus AI memory crunch forces DRAM market into 'hourly pricing' model, report claims SoftBank later established SAIMEMORY in 2024 to commercialize such architectures, moving upstream into memory rather than relying on existing suppliers. Intel joined as a technical partner, while RIKEN supports evaluation and system-level integration. (Image credit: Getty Images / Justin Sullivan)In early 2025, we reported the Intel-SoftBank collaboration to develop a power-efficient HBM alternative for AI data centers through SoftBank's subsidiary, SAIMEMORY. More recently, we also covered Intel’s involvement in co-developing ZAM, a vertically oriented memory concept that promises higher capacity, greater bandwidth, and significantly lower power consumption compared to conventional approaches.The driving force behind all of this is a growing constraint in AI systems: memory.Modern AI workloads demand enormous data throughput between processors and memory. While GPUs have advanced rapidly, memory systems have struggled to keep pace. Today’s standard solution is high-bandwidth memory (HBM) — a form of DRAM (dynamic random access memory) that is vertically stacked and tightly integrated with processors to deliver high speeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsHowever, HBM comes with trade-offs: complex, costly manufacturing; reliance on precise die stacking and bonding; and limited supply dominated by a few vendors -ZAM, a form of DRAM itself, redesigns the structure. Instead of the conventional stacked-and-bonded approach used in HBM, ZAM proposes a vertical memory architecture with a different spatial arrangement and a non-contact, or “wireless,” interconnect between memory layers, thereby improving thermal characteristics by reducing physical constraints.SAIMEMORY claims the design could deliver higher effective density, increased bandwidth, and around 40% lower power consumption compared to traditional HBM. What to read next AMD and Samsung ink memory supply memorandum for EPYC and Instinct products Sambanova introduces new AI accelerator, partners with Intel to deploy Xeon CPUs for inferencing and agentic workloads Memory will consume 30% of hyperscaler data center spending this year, a 4X increase over 2023 If ZAM works, it could directly compete with HBM in a massive and fast-growing market, reduce power consumption in AI data centers for substantial cost savings, and ease supply constraints through a more scalable manufacturing approach.However, the technology remains at an early prototype stage, with a projected path to mass production around 2029. Historically, many “next-generation” memory concepts have failed to progress beyond lab demonstrations, making execution the key uncertainty.The NEDO-supported program is expected to run for approximately 3.5 years, with SAIMEMORY planning to invest around JPY 8 billion (USD 5 million) through fiscal 2027 to develop working prototypes. The longer-term goal is to establish mass production by around 2029.That places ZAM firmly in the next-generation memory cycle, rather than as an immediate replacement for current HBM deployments. In the meantime, incumbent memory manufacturers are continuing to evolve HBM with higher stack counts and improved efficiency.NEDO’s support signifies the Japanese government’s intention to re-enter the chip and semiconductor market — amid skyrocketing AI demand — which it had dominated before Taiwanese and South Korean manufacturers took over market share.“We view the selection of this Project under the NEDO program as a significant milestone in demonstrating Japan-originated next-generation memory technology to the world. ZAM represents an innovative architecture that achieves both the performance and power efficiency required in the AI era and in anticipation of the accelerating AI supercycle. Through collaboration with Intel, RIKEN, and our investors, as well as other domestic and international partners, we aim to contribute to strengthening the global competitiveness of Japan’s semiconductor industry,” Hideya Yamaguchi, President & CEO of SAIMEMORY.SAIMEMORY’s development program is backed by a consortium that includes SoftBank, Fujitsu, RIKEN, and the Development Bank of Japan, alongside government support via NEDO. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 软银子公司与英特尔合作开发革命性ZAM内存，现获日本政府补贴——新内存被设计为面向AI工作负载的低功耗HBM
history.descriptionZh:
软银子公司SAIMEMORY与芯片巨头英特尔合作开发的下一代ZAM（Z-Angle Memory）内存技术，已被日本新能源与产业技术综合开发机构（NEDO）选为“后5G基础设施强化研发项目”的资助对象。这项政府补贴将覆盖项目绝大部分开发成本，标志着日本政府正借助AI需求的爆发式增长，意图重返曾由台湾和韩国主导的半导体市场。

### 背景与上下文：AI内存瓶颈催生技术革新
现代AI工作负载要求在处理器与内存之间实现极高的数据吞吐量。尽管GPU性能快速提升，但内存系统的发展却相对滞后。当前主流解决方案是HBM（高带宽内存）——一种垂直堆叠并与处理器紧密集成的DRAM。然而HBM存在制造复杂、成本高昂、依赖少数供应商等问题，已成为AI系统扩展的关键制约。在这一背景下，软银于2024年成立SAIMEMORY公司，从依赖外部供应商转向自主开发内存架构，英特尔作为技术合作伙伴加入，日本理化学研究所（RIKEN）则支持评估与系统级集成。该项目脱胎于美国政府支持的研究、英特尔内部研发以及软银AI基础设施布局，并基于此前美国主导的互连与内存堆叠技术积累。

### 核心技术原理与创新点：ZAM的垂直架构与“无线”互连
ZAM本身仍属于DRAM，但彻底重构了结构。与HBM传统堆叠加键合（Die Stacking and Bonding）的平面布局不同，ZAM采用垂直内存架构（Vertical Memory Architecture），其空间排列方式完全不同。核心创新在于层间采用“非接触式互连”（Non-contact or “Wireless” Interconnect），即内存层之间通过类似无线的信号传输方式连接，而非物理导线。这种设计大幅减少了物理约束，从而改善散热特性（Thermal Characteristics），并允许更高效的堆叠。

### 性能参数与对比数据
SAIMEMORY声称，与当前传统HBM相比，ZAM能够实现：
- **更高的有效密度**（Higher Effective Density）：在相同物理空间内集成更多内存。
- **更大的带宽**（Increased Bandwidth）：满足AI推理/训练对数据吞吐的持续增长需求。
- **约40%的功耗降低**（Around 40% Lower Power Consumption）：通过非接触互连减少能量损耗，并利用垂直布局优化热管理，降低散热能耗。

### 技术影响与应用场景
若ZAM技术成功，将在以下层面产生显著影响：
1. **市场竞争**：直接挑战HBM在AI数据中心这一快速增长、规模巨大的市场中的主导地位，打破少数供应商垄断。
2. **运营成本**：大幅降低AI数据中心的电力消耗，实现可观的成本节约。
3. **供应链**：采用更可量产的制造方法（垂直架构天然支持更高层数堆叠），缓解当前HBM产能紧张的局面。

ZAM主要针对AI数据中心的加速计算场景，尤其适合对内存带宽、功耗和集成密度有极高要求的训练与推理服务器。

### 当前进展与未来时间线
ZAM目前仍处于早期原型阶段。NEDO资助项目预计持续约3.5年，SAIMEMORY计划在2027财年之前投资约80亿日元（约合500万美元）完成工作原型开发。长期目标是在**2029年左右实现量产**。这意味着ZAM属于下一代内存周期，而非当前HBM部署的即时替代品。在此期间，现有内存制造商将继续通过增加堆叠层数和改进效率来迭代HBM。

SAIMEMORY总裁兼CEO山口英也（Hideya Yamaguchi）表示：“我们视NEDO项目为向世界展示日本原创下一代内存技术的重要里程碑。ZAM是一种实现AI时代所需性能和能效的创新架构，通过英特尔、理研、投资者及国内外合作伙伴的协作，我们将为增强日本半导体产业的全球竞争力做出贡献。”该项目目前获得了软银、富士通、理研、日本政策投资银行以及NEDO政府补贴等多元支持。

history.title: Intel CEO says he can 'think of no better partner than Elon Musk' to explore 'unconventional' ways to improve chip manufacturing — TeraFab partnership aims to rethink how chips are made to reduce costs
history.description: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan commented on the company's partnership with Elon Musk's TeraFab project, but details about the relationship are still light.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan is doubling-down on a partnership with Tesla and SpaceX, which was revealed Wednesday during Tesla's quarterly earnings. On Thursday, during Intel's earnings, Tan said he "could think of no better partner than Elon Musk," vaguely referring to the partnership with the controversial billionaire, who plans to use Intel's upcoming 14A node."Over the last six decades, we have always been willing to take measured risks that have eventually paved the way for step-function improvement in transistor density, cost, power, and performance. As we look to continue challenging the status quo, I can think of no better partner than Elon Musk," said Tan during the open remarks of Intel's quarterly earnings.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingThe exact details of Intel's partnership with Tesla, SpaceX, and the TeraFab project aren't clear. It appears likely that TeraFab will license 14A from Intel, allowing TeraFab to get a node up and running quickly without developing it completely from scratch. However, neither Musk nor Intel has confirmed it's strictly a licensing deal yet.Article continues below You may like Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project Elon Musk says TeraFab will use Intel's 14A process technology to make AI chips Elon Musk unveils $20 billion ‘TeraFab’ chip project to make chips, memory, and package processors all under one roof Tan hinted that the partnership could go beyond a simple licensing arrangement. "Elon and I share a strong conviction that global semiconductor supply is not keeping pace with a rapid acceleration in demand," Tan said during the earnings call. "We are excited to explore innovative ways to refactor silicon process technology, looking for unconventional ways to improve manufacturing efficiency that will eventually lead to a dynamic improvement in the economics of semiconductor manufacturing."TeraFab is the first major customer Intel has scored for 14A, regardless of the specific arrangement. Intel's Foundry business saw declining revenue last year, and it continues operating at a loss, with the company noting a -45% operating margin for Q1 2026, which is up from a low of -71.7% margin in Q2 2025.When pressed directly about the relationship between Intel and TeraFab during the earnings call, Tan didn't share many more details. "We both share the vision that we're going to learn a lot together, exploring the innovative way, and then in the process of the manufacturing. Saying all this, you know, clearly, it's a very broad relationship. And we will update you as we go. Clearly, this is a very exciting customer to work with, and we have multiple other customers we are engaging," Tan said in response.One sticking point about the partnership is the global supply chain not keeping pace with demand, suggesting that Intel and TeraFab will work together in an attempt to shrink the time it takes to hit full production.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIntel's stock was at $66.78 at market close on Thursday, jumping up 19% to $79.74 a share in after-hours trading, likely in response to Intel's comments about its partnership with TeraFab. history.author: Jake Roach
history.titleZh: 英特尔CEO称，在探索改进芯片制造的“非常规”方法方面，“想不出比埃隆·马斯克更好的合作伙伴”——TeraFab合作旨在重新思考芯片制造方式以降低成本
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔CEO陈立武（Lip-Bu Tan）在周三特斯拉季度财报中披露了与特斯拉、SpaceX及埃隆·马斯克旗下TeraFab项目的合作，并在周四英特尔财报电话会议上进一步强调这一战略合作伙伴关系。他公开表示“我想不出比埃隆·马斯克更好的合作伙伴”，暗指这位富有争议的亿万富翁计划采用英特尔即将推出的14A制程节点。

陈立武在财报开场白中指出：“过去60年来，英特尔始终愿意承担经过衡量的风险，这些风险最终为晶体管密度、成本、功耗和性能的阶梯式改进铺平了道路。当我们准备继续挑战现状时，我想不出比埃隆·马斯克更好的合作伙伴。”他暗示双方合作可能远超单纯的授权协议：“埃隆和我都坚信，全球半导体供应跟不上需求的快速加速。我们很兴奋能探索创新方式来重构硅工艺技术，寻找非常规手段提高制造效率，最终实现半导体制造经济性的动态提升。”

TeraFab是英特尔为14A节点争取到的首个重要客户，无论具体合作形式如何，这对英特尔代工业务都意义重大。值得关注的是，英特尔代工部门去年收入持续下滑，且仍处于亏损状态：2026年第一季度的运营利润率为-45%，虽较2025年第二季度的-71.7%有所改善，但整体财务压力依然严峻。当被直接问及英特尔与TeraFab的关系时，陈立武未透露更多细节，仅表示“双方都认同将在探索创新方式和制造过程中共同学习……这是一个非常广泛的关系，我们会随着进展及时更新。显然，这是一个令人兴奋的客户，我们也在与其他多个客户接洽。”

从技术层面看，14A是英特尔计划中的下一代先进制程（Angstrom级别节点），旨在通过新型晶体管架构和材料突破进一步缩小晶体管尺寸，从而提升性能、降低功耗。TeraFab项目由马斯克亲自推进，耗资200亿美元，目标是建立集芯片制造、存储器和处理器封装于一体的超级工厂。如果TeraFab成功获得14A授权，将能快速部署该先进节点生产AI芯片，绕过从零研发新工艺的巨大风险和漫长周期。双方合作的核心驱动力正是全球半导体供应链无法满足AI需求激增的问题，他们希望共同缩短从研发到大规模量产的时间。

这一合作迅速获得资本市场积极反馈：英特尔股票在周四收盘价为66.78美元，盘后交易中飙升19%至79.74美元，反映出投资者对英特尔代工业务有望借助马斯克的产业影响力和AI芯片需求实现转机的强烈预期。尽管具体协议细节（是否仅为授权，还是涉及更深层的技术共建）尚未最终确认，但陈立武的明确表态和股价的暴涨已表明，英特尔正将TeraFab视为扭转代工业务颓势、在AI芯片制造领域与台积电竞争的关键支点。

Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Meta is planning to layoff around 10 percent of employees in May, according to a memo from the company’s chief people officer, Janelle Gale, published by Bloomberg. That means approximately 8,000 people will see their jobs cut. Meta will also be closing around 6,000 open roles, according to Gale.The cuts follow Meta’s significant investments in AI, including spending huge sums to hire top talent and build data centers. The company forecast in January that it will spend $115 billion to $135 billion in capital expenditures in 2026 — a significant increase from its $72.22 billion in capital expenditures for 2025. The increase is to “support our Meta Superintelligence Labs efforts and core business,” Meta said. Earlier this year, Meta announced layoffs affecting hundreds of employees in its recruiting, social media, and sales teams, as well as cuts impacting about 10 percent of its Reality Labs division.Gale wrote that Meta is “doing this as part of our continued effort to run the company more efficiently and to allow us to offset the other investments we’re making. This is not an easy tradeoff and it will mean letting go of people who have made meaningful contributions to Meta during their time here.” Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton confirmed that Bloomberg’s report was accurate but otherwise declined to comment.Affected staffers will be notified on May 20th. “I know this leaves everyone with nearly a month of ambiguity which is incredibly unsettling,” Gale said, noting that “as we’re still working through the details we aren’t able to share much more until later in May.”Reuters reported last week that Meta was targeting May 20th for layoffs, also saying that further cuts are planned for the second half of 2026. In March, Reuters wrote that Meta was considering laying off 20 percent “or more” of the company.
history.author: Jay Peters
history.descriptionZh:
Meta计划在5月裁员约10%，即约8000名员工，同时关闭约6000个空缺职位。这一消息来自Meta首席人事官Janelle Gale发布的内部备忘录，经彭博社披露。此举是Meta持续推进效率提升、为巨额AI投资腾出资源的一部分。Meta在AI领域投入巨大，包括重金招募顶尖人才和建设数据中心。公司1月预测，2026年资本支出将达到1150亿至1350亿美元，较2025年的722.2亿美元大幅增长，主要用于“支持Meta超级智能实验室（Meta Superintelligence Labs）的工作和核心业务”。此前，Meta已在今年早些时候裁撤了招聘、社交媒体和销售团队的数百人，并削减了约10%的Reality Labs部门员工。

Gale在备忘录中表示，裁员是“为了让我们更高效地运营公司，并抵消正在进行其他投资的影响。这不是一个轻松的权衡，它将意味着我们要与那些在Meta期间做出重要贡献的人告别。”Meta发言人Tracy Clayton确认了彭博社报道的准确性，但拒绝进一步评论。

受影响员工将于5月20日收到通知。Gale承认，“我知道这会让所有人面临近一个月的模糊期，这令人非常不安。由于我们仍在处理细节，在5月下旬之前无法透露更多信息。”路透社上周报道，Meta计划在5月20日进行裁员，并可能在2026年下半年进行更多裁员。今年3月，路透社还曾报道Meta考虑裁员20%“或更多”。

此次裁员折射出科技巨头在AI军备竞赛中的残酷取舍：一方面不惜血本押注下一代智能技术，另一方面通过收缩传统业务和优化人力结构来平衡账目。Meta的AI超级智能实验室需要源源不断的资金和算力，而人力成本的削减正是为此铺路。对于被裁员工而言，长达一个月的等待期带来了巨大不确定性，而公司内部已弥漫着对后续裁员计划的担忧。

history.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
history.description: AI agents have revolutionized developer workflows, and their next frontier is knowledge work: processing information, solving complex problems, coming up with new ideas and driving innovation.  Codex, OpenAI’s agentic coding application, is enabling this new frontier. It’s now powered by GPT-5.5, OpenAI’s latest frontier model, which runs on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack-scale systems.  Over 10,000 […]
history.content: AI agents have revolutionized developer workflows, and their next frontier is knowledge work: processing information, solving complex problems, coming up with new ideas and driving innovation. Codex, OpenAI’s agentic coding application, is enabling this new frontier. It’s now powered by GPT-5.5, OpenAI’s latest frontier model, which runs on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack-scale systems. Over 10,000 NVIDIANs — across engineering, product, legal, marketing, finance, sales, HR, operations and developer programs — are already using GPT-5.5-powered Codex to achieve, in their words, “mind-blowing” and “life-changing” results. NVIDIA engineers have had access to GPT-5.5 through the Codex app for a few weeks, and the gains are measurable. Served on GB200 NVL72, which is capable of delivering 35x lower cost per million tokens and 50x higher token output per second per megawatt compared with prior-generation systems — economics that make frontier-model inference viable at enterprise scale. Debugging cycles that once stretched across days are closing in hours. Experimentation that previously required weeks is turning into overnight progress in complex, multi-file codebases. Teams are shipping end-to-end features from natural-language prompts, with stronger reliability and fewer wasted cycles than earlier models. OpenAI’s stunning progress is just the latest example of NVIDIA’s work with every frontier model company — not just to accelerate the use of AI agents inside NVIDIA, but to help the company’s partners build the world’s best, lowest cost and most power efficient models for everyone. As NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang told employees in a company-wide email urging everyone to use Codex: “Let’s jump to lightspeed. Welcome to the age of AI.” A Deployment Built for Enterprise Security Just like humans, every agent needs its own dedicated computer. To ensure seamless operation within secure enterprise environments, the Codex app supports remote Secure Shell (SSH) connections to approved cloud virtual machines, allowing agents to work with real company data without exposing it externally. So to ensure maximum security and auditability, NVIDIA IT rolled out cloud virtual machines (VMs) for every employee to run their agent safely. This provides a dedicated sandbox for the agent to operate at its maximum capabilities while maintaining full auditability. Users can control the Codex agent running in the cloud VM from a user interface that every employee is familiar with. A zero-data retention policy governs NVIDIA’s deployment, and agents access production systems with read-only permissions through command-line interfaces and Skills — the same agentic toolkit NVIDIA uses to run automation workflows across the company. A Decade of Full-Stack Collaboration The GPT-5.5 launch and the Codex rollout reflect more than 10 years of collaboration between NVIDIA and OpenAI. The partnership began in 2016, when Huang hand-delivered the first NVIDIA DGX-1 AI supercomputer to OpenAI’s San Francisco headquarters. Since then, the two companies have worked closely across the full AI stack. NVIDIA was a day-zero partner for OpenAI’s gpt-oss open-weight model launch, optimizing model weights for NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM and ecosystem frameworks including vLLM and Ollama. OpenAI has committed to deploying more than 10 gigawatts of NVIDIA systems for its next-generation AI infrastructure — a buildout that will put millions of NVIDIA GPUs at the foundation of OpenAI’s model training and inference for years ahead. And OpenAI and NVIDIA are early silicon and codesign partners: OpenAI provides feedback that informs NVIDIA’s hardware roadmap, and in turn gains early access to new architectures. That relationship produced a concrete milestone — the joint bring-up of the first GB200 NVL72 100,000-GPU cluster. The cluster completed multiple large-scale training runs and set a new benchmark for system-level reliability at frontier scale. GPT-5.5 is the product of that infrastructure running at full strength. Learn more in OpenAI’s announcement.
history.author: Justin Boitano
history.titleZh: OpenAI的新GPT-5.5在NVIDIA基础设施上驱动Codex——而NVIDIA已将其投入应用
history.descriptionZh:
AI代理已经彻底改变了开发者工作流，而它们的前沿领域正转向知识工作：处理信息、解决复杂问题、提出新想法并推动创新。OpenAI的代理编程应用Codex正在开拓这一新领域，它目前由OpenAI最新前沿模型GPT-5.5驱动，运行在NVIDIA GB200 NVL72机架级系统上。超过1万名NVIDIA员工——涵盖工程、产品、法律、营销、财务、销售、人力资源、运营和开发者计划等部门——已经在使用基于GPT-5.5的Codex，并称其取得了“令人震撼”和“改变人生”的效果。

NVIDIA工程师通过Codex应用访问GPT-5.5已有数周，成效显著。GB200 NVL72系统相比前代产品，每百万Token成本降低35倍，每兆瓦功耗下每秒Token输出量提升50倍——这种经济性使得前沿模型推理在企业规模下变得可行。原本需要数天的调试周期现在缩短至数小时；原本需要数周的实验，在复杂多文件代码库中只需一夜就能取得进展。团队通过自然语言提示即可交付端到端功能，且相比早期模型可靠性更强、浪费更少。

OpenAI的惊人进展只是NVIDIA与所有前沿模型公司合作的最近案例——不仅加速AI代理在NVIDIA内部的应用，还帮助合作伙伴构建全球最佳、成本最低、能效最高的模型。正如NVIDIA创始人兼CEO黄仁勋在致全体员工的邮件中所说：“让我们跃入光速。欢迎来到AI时代。”

**专为企业安全打造的部署方案**  
正如人类需要专属计算机，每个代理也需要独立的计算环境。为确保在安全的企业环境中无缝运行，Codex应用支持通过远程安全Shell（SSH）连接到经批准的云端虚拟机，使代理能够处理真实公司数据而无需对外暴露。为最大程度确保安全性和可审计性，NVIDIA IT部门为每位员工部署了云端虚拟机，用于安全运行代理。这为代理提供了专用沙盒，使其在充分发挥能力的同时保持完全可审计。用户可通过每位员工都熟悉的用户界面控制运行在云端虚拟机中的Codex代理。NVIDIA的部署采用零数据保留政策，代理通过命令行界面和“Skills”（即NVIDIA在公司范围内用于运行自动化工作流的同一代理工具包）以只读权限访问生产系统。

**十年全栈协作**  
GPT-5.5的发布和Codex的推广，反映了NVIDIA与OpenAI超过十年的合作。双方合作始于2016年，当时黄仁勋亲手将第一台NVIDIA DGX-1 AI超级计算机送到OpenAI旧金山总部。此后，两家公司在AI全栈上紧密合作。NVIDIA是OpenAI开源权重模型gpt-oss的零日合作伙伴，为其权重针对NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM及包括vLLM和Ollama在内的生态系统框架进行优化。OpenAI已承诺部署超过10吉瓦的NVIDIA系统用于其下一代AI基础设施——这一建设将使数百万块NVIDIA GPU在未来数年成为OpenAI模型训练和推理的基础。此外，OpenAI和NVIDIA是早期硅片和协同设计合作伙伴：OpenAI提供反馈以影响NVIDIA硬件路线图，同时获得新架构的早期访问权。这一关系产生了一个具体里程碑——首个GB200 NVL72 10万GPU集群的联合启动。该集群完成了多次大规模训练运行，并创下了前沿规模下系统级可靠性的新基准。GPT-5.5正是该基础设施全力运行的产物。
